paper_metadata.title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations
paper_metadata.abstract: Increasing model size when pretraining natural language representations often results in improved performance on downstream tasks. However, at some point further model increases become harder due to GPU/TPU memory limitations and longer training times. To address these problems, we present two parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase the training speed of BERT. Comprehensive empirical evidence shows that our proposed methods lead to models that scale much better compared to the original BERT. We also use a self-supervised loss that focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence, and show it consistently helps downstream tasks with multi-sentence inputs. As a result, our best model establishes new state-of-the-art results on the GLUE, RACE, and \squad benchmarks while having fewer parameters compared to BERT-large. The code and the pretrained models are available at https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT.

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces two parameter-reduction techniques: factorized embedding parameterization and cross-layer parameter sharing. These reduce model parameters significantly while improving training speed and generalization. Additionally, it replaces Next Sentence Prediction (NSP) with Sentence Order Prediction (SOP) to better model inter-sentence coherence.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM), Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (implied from BERT context; not explicitly named in this paper)
extraction_data.extension: Factorized embedding parameterization and cross-layer parameter sharing reduce model size while maintaining performance.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient language representation learning with reduced model size and improved training efficiency
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering, reading comprehension
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces factorized embedding parameterization and cross-layer parameter sharing to reduce model parameters while maintaining performance. It replaces BERT's next-sentence prediction (NSP) with a more challenging inter-sentence coherence loss based on predicting the order of two consecutive text segments.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (implied from BERT context; not explicitly named in this paper)
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering, sentence pair tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Parameter efficiency and improved language representation learning
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM), Inter-sentence coherence loss
extraction_data.extension: Factorized embedding parameterization and cross-layer parameter sharing reduce model size while maintaining performance.
